text.title: AI Video Generation Provider Selection Core Rules

rule.statement: Choose Runway Gen-3 when the task requires a reliable production API, video-to-video transformations, lip-sync for talking heads, or precise motion control.
rule.rationale: Runway is the only 2026 provider with a stable public REST API supporting all four simultaneously.

rule.statement: Choose Luma Dream Machine when the task is simple text-to-video, image animation, video extension, or is budget-conscious.
rule.rationale: Luma credits are typically cheaper per second than Runway; it supports extension which Sora 2 does not.

rule.statement: Choose Sora 2 when long-form (up to 60s) photorealistic clips are required and a ChatGPT Pro subscription is available.
rule.rationale: Sora 2 produces the best character consistency and the longest single-call duration; API is gated to ChatGPT Pro/Plus.

rule.statement: Choose Veo 3 when 4K resolution or native audio is required and Google Vertex AI infrastructure is available.
rule.rationale: Veo 3 is the only provider with native audio generation; 4K via Vertex is enterprise-gated.

rule.statement: Choose Replicate when open-source models, custom fine-tunes, or self-hosted cost optimisation are needed.
rule.rationale: Replicate hosts 50+ open video models with per-generation pricing and self-host option.

rule.statement: Every selected provider MUST be wired through async polling (60-300s generation); never block a request thread.
rule.rationale: All commercial APIs are async; blocking causes worker pool starvation.
